summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #279 — a ScrollArea agnostic core state machine for the ars-ui Rust component library, following existing patterns for state/event/effect/API structure.
summary.outcome: ScrollArea core implemented (~900 lines), wired into layout module, 56 passing tests, 3 Codex review passes completed with all 14 P2 threads fixed and resolved, CI passing, PR #699 opened — pending final thread replies/resolve and Codex re-trigger confirmation for pass 3.

repo: The Effect intent pattern (e.g., Effect::AutoHide) is the established convention for async/timer side-effects in agnostic machines — not inline platform timers. Reference implementations: tooltip and rating_group.
Props that represent durations use Duration type, not u32/ms integers — consistent with tooltip, hover_card, and avatar conventions.

The canonical RTL scroll normalization helper is ars_collections::normalize_scroll_left_rtl(RtlScrollMode) in crates/ars-collections/src/virtualization.rs — local reimplementations are error-prone (sign-heuristic breaks at raw==0 for positive mode).
on_props_changed must be implemented via a SyncProps event + trait method, matching the collapsible pattern — omitting it is a known review catch.
cargo xci-fast runs 10 CI gates and is the fast pre-push check.

code.finding: Visibility gating by orientation (allows_x/allows_y) belongs in the core machine via Context.orientation + ScrollOrientation helpers, not pushed to adapter layer.
SyncProps event and on_props_changed trait method were missing from initial implementation — required to handle live prop updates, matching collapsible pattern.
Hover state could get stuck after root-leave + scrollbar-leave sequence; fix requires Context.hovering_root tracking to disambiguate compound hover regions.
new_thumb_pos was clamped at 0 but not at the upper bound; must clamp to [0, track_size - thumb_size] to prevent thumb overrun.
Resize with non-overflowing axes did not clear visibility flags when in Hover or Scroll modes — requires explicit flag reset on resize.
ThumbDragEnd in Scroll mode never restarted the AutoHide timer — must emit Effect::AutoHide after drag ends in Scroll visibility mode.
normalize_scroll_left_rtl takes explicit RtlScrollMode (adapter-detected, not sign-heuristic). Sign-heuristic approach fails at raw==0 for positive scroll mode.
Pre-existing clippy lint: map_or_else(.., identity) flagged by stable toolchain 1.96.0 (released 4 days prior). Fixed incidentally during this session.

workflow: context_only closures in this codebase require explicit &mut Context type annotations — the compiler does not infer them, causing multiple build fix rounds.
Messages structs require PartialEq derive to compile — easy to miss when scaffolding new components.
Codex review passes are iterative; plan for at least 2-3 passes with P2 threads requiring TDD fixes and spec sync before threads can be resolved.
Spec, impl, and all adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) must be kept in sync — omitting adapter spec updates is a review catch.
State enums should derive Copy per project spec conventions — easy to overlook during initial scaffolding.
When fixing Codex threads: implement with TDD, sync spec, reply to thread, resolve thread, then re-trigger Codex — all steps are required.

friction: Spec for issue #279 contained an embedded platform set_timeout timer inside the agnostic machine — required design correction before implementation could begin.
Axis enum was missing from the spec entirely and needed to be defined from scratch.
Multiple rounds of compiler errors due to missing PartialEq on Messages and missing explicit &mut Context annotations in context_only closures.
Snapshot test acceptance was blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated fixtures unrelated to this change.
Background job races during snapshot regeneration caused non-deterministic test behavior.
New stable toolchain (1.96.0) introduced a clippy regression on pre-existing pagination code, requiring an incidental fix.
hide_delay_ms: u32 was initially implemented before user corrected it to hide_delay: Duration — convention not documented in spec, discovered via feedback.
open_items: Codex pass 3 thread replies, thread resolution, and Codex re-trigger were not confirmed complete at transcript end — needs follow-up.

normalize_scroll_left local helper removed and deferred to ars_collections::normalize_scroll_left_rtl — adapter layer must detect and supply RtlScrollMode, which is not yet implemented in any adapter.
scrollbar_cross_size prop was added to Leptos/Dioxus adapter spec sketches but full adapter implementations were not written (out of scope for this issue).
Local Axis{X,Y} enum differs from ars-dom's Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} — identified as a potential future cross-cutting refactor, explicitly deferred.
